feature: Pipeline Status Dashboard with Real-time Progress

tasks.task: Analyse story requirements and produce a concrete implementation plan for the real-time pipeline status dashboard
tasks.output: Three files to change: src/types.ts (add started_at/completed_at to Task), src/panels/BuilderPanel.ts (send logs, enhance renderWorkflows), src/extension.ts (add BuilderPanel to onLogChange)

tasks.task: Design the enhanced step-card layout: status icons, agent label, elapsed time, active-log strip, and progress bar label
tasks.output: Step boxes gain status CSS classes (step-pending/in_progress/done/failed), spinner keyframe, log strip below step row, progress label beside bar

tasks.task: Write acceptance test scenarios that map to each AC in the story
tasks.output: 5 scenarios defined covering: real-time refresh, in_progress spinner+log, done checkmark+elapsed, failed error, final progress bar at 100%

tasks.task: Apply code changes to types.ts, BuilderPanel.ts, and extension.ts
tasks.output: types.ts: +started_at/completed_at on Task. BuilderPanel.ts: import readLogEntries, send logs in _sendAllData, add status CSS, rewrite renderWorkflows with task status per step/progress bar/log strip. extension.ts: BuilderPanel.refreshAll added to onLogChange.

tasks.task: Review diff against story ACs and check for regressions
tasks.output: All 5 ACs satisfied. No regressions: existing tabs (Agents, Skills, Epics, History) untouched. No polling introduced.

tasks.task: Run npm run compile; verify zero TypeScript errors
tasks.output: npm run compile exited 0, zero errors

tasks.task: No deployment step for a VS Code extension change — mark complete after compile passes
tasks.output: Extension ships on next manual package/publish cycle
Grep confirms BuilderPanel.refreshAll is called in both onBoardChange (line 34) and onLogChange (line 40) in extension.ts. No polling.
